parsed_review.summary: CP00-459 is a clean, well-bounded descriptor-only analytics-core increment (RP15.P03 permission fixture slice: M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail of 4 rows + M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head of 6 rows = 10 units, risk A). Pack identity and plan binding are consistent across manifest, review-baseline, contract current_pack, registry CP459 binding, and closeout-pack-plan.json (range RP15.P03.M05.S17-RP15.P03.M06.S06; deliverables impl:6/contract:2/security_audit:2; micro-titles Permission And Audit Binding:4/Synthetic Fixture Set:6; deviation_from_plan:false). Implementation in registry.js/service.js/validators.js/index.js follows the established CP453-CP458 descriptor pattern: every row carries descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false, and the no-write attestation sets all runtime/write/leak flags false (no analytics/Matter P&L/forecast/WIP runtime, no state/audit/permission writes, no real fixture data, no Hermes runtime receipt). The contract p03_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor projection matches the generated descriptor exactly (deepEqual-enforced by the validator script), and the authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime opening deferred to CP00-460. Tests (3 dedicated CP459 cases plus the rp15 contract-validation script) and command-evidence (all 27 commands exit 0, Hermes H15 gate passed) support the claim; Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic no-write review packet/receipt evidence (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening checks pass: readiness auth-preflight succeeded with valid non-empty JSON stdout (1432 bytes, 0 stderr), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob (forbidden_tools_present:false), forbidden_review_evidence list present, and post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json/adjudication.md/construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent in the pre-review state. The pack-level production_ready boolean is false (implementation_layer:descriptor, runtime_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review); it claims only descriptor verification via production_ready_flag and does not over-claim runtime production readiness or enterprise trust, with Claude remaining a read-only reviewer rather than final approver. Unrelated dirty/untracked files are correctly preserved and not inferred as pack scope. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
